title: Fluentd Buffer Overflow and Retry Exhaustion Events
app: Fluentd deployment with file/system buffer, log forwarder to Splunk HEC
spl:
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentd:log"
| search "buffer" AND ("overflow" OR "chunk full" OR "retry limit" OR "giving up" OR "emit error")
| stats count, latest(_time) as t, values(plugin_id) as plugins by host, buffer_path
| where count >= 5
| sort -count
description: When Fluentd cannot flush faster than inputs arrive, chunks block and events are dropped or stalled—often the first sign of downstream indexer saturation. Tracking overflow and retry-exhaustion strings prioritizes the correct output plugin to fix.
value: Prevents silent log loss during traffic spikes and gives SREs a faster cue than disk-full pages alone.
implementation: Tag `plugin_id` from structured logs when possible. Correlate with Splunk indexer queue KPIs. Tune `retry_forever` and secondary destinations per vendor guidance.
visualization: Event timeline, bar chart (host × plugin), single-value (open buffer faults).
grandmaExplanation: We read the forwarder and buffer logs for stress and give-ups so we see log loss risk before disks or patience run out.
knownFalsePositives: Intake blips can follow token or load-balancer work and client bursts. We align with app owners before a platform-wide incident.
